title: woodsia pusilla var. mexicana
intro: Woodsia pusilla var. mexicana is a variety of the Woodsia pusilla fern, a type of small, delicate fern native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing swelling. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Woodsia pusilla var. mexicana can be made by infusing the dried leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
